text: How do you explain when things don't go as we expect? Or better, how do you explain when others achieve things that seem impossible? Why is Apple so innovative every year? They're just a computer company with the same access to talent, agencies, and consultants as their competitors.
sentences: How do you explain when things don't go as we expect?
Or better, how do you explain when others achieve things that seem impossible?
Why is Apple so innovative every year?
They're just a computer company with the same access to talent, agencies, and consultants as their competitors.

text: Why did Martin Luther King lead the Civil Rights Movement when others faced the same struggles? Why did the Wright brothers achieve powered flight when better-funded teams failed? There's something different happening. About three and a half years ago, I noticed a pattern.
sentences: Why did Martin Luther King lead the Civil Rights Movement when others faced the same struggles?
Why did the Wright brothers achieve powered flight when better-funded teams failed?
There's something different happening.
About three and a half years ago, I noticed a pattern.

text: All great inspiring leaders and organizations think, act, and communicate the same way—the opposite of everyone else. I call it the golden circle: Why? How? What?
sentences: All great inspiring leaders and organizations think, act, and communicate the same way—the opposite of everyone else.
I call it the golden circle: Why?
How?
What?

text: Every organization knows what they do. Some know how they do it. But very few know why. By why, I don't mean profit—that's a result.
sentences: Every organization knows what they do.
Some know how they do it.
But very few know why.
By why, I don't mean profit—that's a result.

text: I mean your purpose, cause, and belief. Why does your organization exist? Why do you get out of bed? Most people communicate from the outside in—from what to how to why.
sentences: I mean your purpose, cause, and belief.
Why does your organization exist?
Why do you get out of bed?
Most people communicate from the outside in—from what to how to why.

text: Inspired leaders communicate inside out. If Apple communicated like everyone else, they'd say: "We make great computers. They're beautifully designed and user-friendly. Want to buy one?" Instead, Apple says: "Everything we do, we believe in challenging the status quo and thinking differently.
sentences: Inspired leaders communicate inside out.
If Apple communicated like everyone else, they'd say: "We make great computers.
They're beautifully designed and user-friendly.
Want to buy one?" Instead, Apple says: "Everything we do, we believe in challenging the status quo and thinking differently.

text:
We make beautiful, simple products. We happen to make computers. Want to buy one?"

People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it. This explains why people buy iPhones, MP3 players, and phones from Apple, yet won't buy these from Dell or Gateway—competitors equally qualified to make them.
sentences: We make beautiful, simple products.
We happen to make computers.

Want to buy one?"

People don't buy what you do; they buy why you do it.
This explains why people buy iPhones, MP3 players, and phones from Apple, yet won't buy these from Dell or Gateway—competitors equally qualified to make them.

text: This isn't opinion; it's based on biology. The human brain has three major parts that match the golden circle. The neocortex handles rational, analytical thought and language. The limbic brain controls feelings like trust and loyalty, plus all behavior and decision-making—with no language capacity.
sentences: This isn't opinion; it's based on biology.
The human brain has three major parts that match the golden circle.
The neocortex handles rational, analytical thought and language.
The limbic brain controls feelings like trust and loyalty, plus all behavior and decision-making—with no language capacity.

text: When we communicate inside out, we speak directly to the limbic brain, which controls decisions. People rationalize with tangible facts afterward. This is why people say decisions "feel right" even with contradicting data. Consider the Wright brothers versus Samuel Pierpont Langley.
sentences: When we communicate inside out, we speak directly to the limbic brain, which controls decisions.
People rationalize with tangible facts afterward.
This is why people say decisions "feel right" even with contradicting data.
Consider the Wright brothers versus Samuel Pierpont Langley.

text: Langley had fifty thousand dollars from the War Department, Harvard credentials, Smithsonian connections, and top talent. Media followed him everywhere. The Wright brothers had no money, no college education, no media attention. Yet they succeeded.
sentences: Langley had fifty thousand dollars from the War Department, Harvard credentials, Smithsonian connections, and top talent.
Media followed him everywhere.
The Wright brothers had no money, no college education, no media attention.
Yet they succeeded.

text: The difference? The Wrights were driven by purpose and belief that flight would change the world. Langley pursued wealth and fame—the results. Their team worked with blood, sweat, and tears.
sentences: The difference?
The Wrights were driven by purpose and belief that flight would change the world.
Langley pursued wealth and fame—the results.
Their team worked with blood, sweat, and tears.

text: When the Wrights flew on December 17, 1903, nobody was there. Langley quit immediately. If you talk about what you believe, you attract those who believe what you believe. This connects to diffusion of innovation theory.
sentences: When the Wrights flew on December 17, 1903, nobody was there.
Langley quit immediately.
If you talk about what you believe, you attract those who believe what you believe.
This connects to diffusion of innovation theory.

text: The first 2.5 percent are innovators. The next 13.5 percent are early adopters. Then comes the early majority, late majority, and laggards. Mass-market success requires reaching 15-18 percent penetration.
sentences: The first 2.5 percent are innovators.
The next 13.5 percent are early adopters.
Then comes the early majority, late majority, and laggards.
Mass-market success requires reaching 15-18 percent penetration.

text: Most businesses achieve about 10 percent conversion—those who "get it." Early adopters buy based on belief, not product features. They stood in line for iPhones and paid forty thousand dollars for early flat-screen TVs. TiVo failed commercially despite being the highest-quality product, well-funded, with fantastic market conditions. They told people what they had.
sentences: Most businesses achieve about 10 percent conversion—those who "get it." Early adopters buy based on belief, not product features.
They stood in line for iPhones and paid forty thousand dollars for early flat-screen TVs.
TiVo failed commercially despite being the highest-quality product, well-funded, with fantastic market conditions.
They told people what they had.

text: People said they didn't need it. Had they said, "If you like total control over your life, we have a product for you," the outcome might differ. Dr. Martin Luther King succeeded differently.
sentences: People said they didn't need it.
Had they said, "If you like total control over your life, we have a product for you," the outcome might differ.
Dr.
Martin Luther King succeeded differently.

text: In summer 1963, 250,000 people showed up at Washington's mall with no invitations or website. King didn't tell people what needed changing; he shared his beliefs. People who believed what he believed made his cause their own. They didn't come for him—they came for themselves.
sentences: In summer 1963, 250,000 people showed up at Washington's mall with no invitations or website.
King didn't tell people what needed changing; he shared his beliefs.
People who believed what he believed made his cause their own.
They didn't come for him—they came for themselves.

text: King believed in two types of laws: those from higher authority and those from men. Justice requires alignment. He gave the "I have a dream" speech, not an "I have a plan" speech. Politicians now offer comprehensive plans that inspire nobody.
sentences: King believed in two types of laws: those from higher authority and those from men.
Justice requires alignment.
He gave the "I have a dream" speech, not an "I have a plan" speech.
Politicians now offer comprehensive plans that inspire nobody.

text: Leaders hold position or authority. Those who lead inspire us. We follow them not because we must, but because we want to. We follow those who start with "why"—those with the ability to inspire others or find those who inspire them.
sentences: Leaders hold position or authority.
Those who lead inspire us.
We follow them not because we must, but because we want to.
We follow those who start with "why"—those with the ability to inspire others or find those who inspire them.
